generatedSentence: The dandified paymaster, with his handsome but pawky grin, had achieved an effable reputation across the roundabout of high-stakes interviews. His jolly vacations were legendary, often featuring pictures of exotic juniper vegetation as a backdrop to his largesse. He'd once plonked a giant billboard declaring "No Worms Here!" near the ringside of a particularly challenging negotiation, causing many humans to readjust their phonetic assumptions. But a devilish scam began to unravel, a complex rollover involving mortgage fraud and jails that threatened his carefully orchestrated legacy. A glitch in the system, a deep undercut to their financial bandwidth, revealed he was an unresponsive executor, pushing him to his kneecap. The heavens, like giant owls, seemed to screech a warning as the forecast promised a drumhead court-martial, making him abashed and causing many guffaws. The ballroom, filled with mohawks and knitted brows, became a territory of conflict as the truth about the missing corpse emerged. He lurched forward, his veins throbbing, a talkative gadfly trying to rally support, but the heavyweight prosecutor was relentless. The keyways to his defense were sealed; he was being lynched by public opinion, the whole thing a schlocky spectacle that felt like a tachyon blur. At dawn, after the last ante was called, he was seen kneeling in the lowland, a duckling among lions, licking his wounds. He accepted his new fate, the past reduced to redacted histories, a jellybean memory. With a newborn outlook, he would travel to a condominium, nowhere near the previous corruption, seeking nutrition and perhaps a quiet marriage, forever haunted by the micro-glitches and the purple accent of his downfall.

His jolly vacations were legendary, often featuring pictures of exotic juniper vegetation as a backdrop to his largesse. But a devilish scam began to unravel, a complex rollover involving mortgage fraud and jails that threatened his carefully orchestrated legacy. The keyways to his defense were sealed; he was being lynched by public opinion, the whole thing a schlocky spectacle that felt like a tachyon blur.

rawPanelSentences: The dandified paymaster, with his handsome but pawky grin, had achieved an effable reputation across the roundabout of high-stakes interviews. His jolly vacations were legendary, often featuring pictures of exotic juniper vegetation as a backdrop to his largesse. He'd once plonked a giant billboard declaring "No Worms Here!" near the ringside of a particularly challenging negotiation, causing many humans to readjust their phonetic assumptions.

But a devilish scam began to unravel, a complex rollover involving mortgage fraud and jails that threatened his carefully orchestrated legacy. A glitch in the system, a deep undercut to their financial bandwidth, revealed he was an unresponsive executor, pushing him to his kneecap. The heavens, like giant owls, seemed to screech a warning as the forecast promised a drumhead court-martial, making him abashed and causing many guffaws.

The ballroom, filled with mohawks and knitted brows, became a territory of conflict as the truth about the missing corpse emerged. He lurched forward, his veins throbbing, a talkative gadfly trying to rally support, but the heavyweight prosecutor was relentless. The keyways to his defense were sealed; he was being lynched by public opinion, the whole thing a schlocky spectacle that felt like a tachyon blur.

With a newborn outlook, he would travel to a condominium, nowhere near the previous corruption, seeking nutrition and perhaps a quiet marriage, forever haunted by the micro-glitches and the purple accent of his downfall.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a sharply dressed, heavyset man with a single, perfectly coiffed purple streak in his hair, wearing a tailored suit with subtle glowing circuitry patterns. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist, looking confident and slightly smug, stands before a massive, glowing billboard in a bustling, futuristic city square, surrounded by various alien humanoids, wide-angle shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist, now looking stressed and abashed, is being confronted by a shadowy, horned figure (the 'devil'), while digital 'worms' and 'glitches' flow around them, close-up on their faces. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist, on one knee in a futuristic, ornate ballroom, surrounded by stern-faced individuals with mohawks and intricate knitted patterns on their clothing, a 'corpse' outlined on the floor, low-angle shot from behind the protagonist. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist, weary but resolute, is walking towards a minimalist, glowing condominium at dawn, a small 'duckling' robot companion trailing him, distant wide shot.

definitions.definition: The width, usually measured in hertz, of a frequency band.
Of a signal, the width of the smallest frequency band within which the signal can fit.
The rate of data flow in digital networks typically measured in bits per second; the bitrate.
The capacity, energy or time required.
definitions.example: I think it's a worthy project, but I just don't have the bandwidth right now.

definitions.definition: A decorated cloth hung at the back of a stage.
An image that serves as a visual background.
The setting or background of an acted performance.
Any background situation.
definitions.example: Against a backdrop of falling interest rates, the new savings account is looking less appealing.

definitions.definition: To serve as a backdrop for.
definitions.example: a brilliant sunset backdropping the famous skyline

definitions.definition: An evil creature.
(with article) The chief devil; Satan.

A fictional image of a man, usually red or orange in skin color; with a set of horns on his head, a pointed goatee and a long tail and carrying a pitchfork; that represents evil and portrayed to children in an effort to discourage bad behavior.
The bad part of the conscience; the opposite to the angel.
definitions.example: The devil in me wants to let him suffer.

definitions.definition: A wicked or naughty person, or one who harbors reckless, spirited energy, especially in a mischievous way; usually said of a young child.
definitions.example: Those two kids are devils in a toy store.

definitions.definition: A thing that is awkward or difficult to understand or do.
definitions.example: That math problem was a devil.

definitions.definition: (with an article, as an intensifier) Hell.
definitions.example: She is having a devil of a time fixing it.

definitions.definition: A person, especially a man; used to express a particular opinion of him, usually in the phrases poor devil and lucky devil.
A dust devil.
(Christian Science) An evil or erring entity.
(in compounds) A barren, unproductive and unused area.
definitions.example: devil strip

definitions.definition: A dish, as a bone with the meat, broiled and excessively peppered; a grill with Cayenne pepper.
A machine for tearing or cutting rags, cotton, etc.
A Tasmanian devil.
An endurance event where riders who fall behind are periodically eliminated.
To make like a devil; to invest with the character of a devil.
To annoy or bother.
To work as a ‘devil’; to work for a lawyer or writer without fee or recognition.
To prepare (food) with spices, making it spicy:
The morning twilight period immediately before sunrise.
The rising of the sun.
The time when the sun rises.
definitions.example: She rose before dawn to meet the train.

definitions.definition: The earliest phase of something.
definitions.example: the dawn of civilization

definitions.definition: To begin to brighten with daylight.
definitions.example: A new day dawns.

definitions.definition: To start to appear or be realized.
definitions.example: I don’t want to be there when the truth dawns on him.

definitions.definition: To begin to give promise; to begin to appear or to expand.
Characteristic (in dress and habits) of a dandy
To dress as, or to adopt the style of, a dandy.

The thin circle of material attached to the top of a drum shell for the purpose of striking, sometimes made of skin and in such occurrences sometimes referred to as a skin, or drum-skin, but often synthetic.
A drumhead cabbage.
A fraudulent deal.
definitions.example: That marketing scheme looks like a scam to me.

definitions.definition: Something that is promoted using scams.
definitions.example: That car was a scam.

definitions.definition: To defraud or embezzle.
definitions.example: They tried to scam her out of her savings.

definitions.definition: A pleasure trip or excursion.
A marine in the English navy.
To amuse or divert.
Full of merriment and high spirits; jovial.
Full of life and mirth; jouous; merry.
Splendid, excellent, pleasant
Large
Very, extremely
(Australia and sometimes United States) A road junction at which traffic streams circularly around a central island.
A horizontal wheel which rotates around a central axis when pushed and on which children ride, often found in parks as a children's play apparatus.
A fairground carousel.
A detour.
A short, close-fitting coat or jacket worn by men or boys, especially in the 19th century.
A round dance.
Indirect, circuitous, or circumlocutionary.
Encircling; enveloping; comprehensive.
The process of incrementing, especially back to an initial value.
A road traffic accident in which a vehicle overturns.
A graphic element that changes its appearance when the cursor moves over it.
A keyboard feature where each key is scanned independently, so that multiple simultaneous keypresses always register correctly.
The reinvestment of funds in a new issue of the same or similar investment.
A fee paid by a borrower in order to defer full repayment of a loan.
In the National or European lottery, the situation in which a jackpot that has not been won is carried over to the next week.
A target on the pinball table that is activated when the ball rolls over it.
Area beside a ring.
Beside the ring, especially a boxing ring.
definitions.example: We got our hands on a pair of ringside tickets for the boxing match.

definitions.definition: To adjust again
Freedom from some business or activity.
Free time given over to a specific purpose; occupation, activity.
A period during which official activity or business is formally suspended; an official holiday from university, law courts etc.
A holiday; a stretch of leisure time away from work or duty and devoted to rest or pleasure.
The act of vacating something; moving out.
definitions.example: The Conservative Party’s vacation of the centre ground gave an opportunity to its opponents.

definitions.definition: The act of making legally void.
To spend or take a vacation.
definitions.example: This year, we’re vacationing in Mexico.

definitions.definition: To render handsome.
(of people, things, etc) Having a good appearance; good-looking.
definitions.example: a handsome garment, house, tree, or horse

definitions.definition: Good, appealing, appropriate.
Generous or noble in character.
definitions.example: Handsome is as handsome does.

definitions.definition: Ample; moderately large.
definitions.example: a handsome salary

definitions.definition: (said of things and people) Dexterous; skillful.
An official face-to-face meeting of monarchs or other important figures.
Any face-to-face meeting, especially of an official nature.
A conversation in person (or, by extension, over the telephone, Internet etc.) between a journalist and someone whose opinion or statements he or she wishes to record for publication, broadcast etc.
definitions.example: The reporter gave the witness an interview.

definitions.definition: A formal meeting, in person, for the assessment of a candidate or applicant.
definitions.example: It was a dreadful interview; I have no hope of getting the job.

definitions.definition: An audition.
A police interrogation of a suspect or party in an investigation.
To ask questions of (somebody); to have an interview.
definitions.example: He interviewed the witness.

definitions.definition: To be interviewed; to attend an interview.
To set or toss (something) down carelessly.
definitions.example: When you’ve finished with the sponge, just plonk it back in the sink.

definitions.definition: To automatically ignore a particular poster.
definitions.example: I got tired of his trolling and ad hominem attacks, so I plonked him.

definitions.definition: Shrewd, sly; often also characterised by a sarcastic sense of humour.
A representation of anything (as a person, a landscape, a building) upon canvas, paper, or other surface, by drawing, painting, printing, photography, etc.
An image; a representation as in the imagination.
A painting.
definitions.example: There was a picture hanging above the fireplace.

definitions.definition: A photograph.
definitions.example: I took a picture of the church.

definitions.definition: A motion picture.
definitions.example: Casablanca is my all-time favorite picture.

definitions.definition: (in the plural) ("the pictures") Cinema (as a form of entertainment).
definitions.example: Let's go to the pictures.

definitions.definition: A paragon, a perfect example or specimen (of a category).
definitions.example: She's the very picture of health.

definitions.definition: An attractive sight.
definitions.example: The garden is a real picture at this time of year.

definitions.definition: The art of painting; representation by painting.
A figure; a model.
Situation.
definitions.example: The employment picture for the older middle class is not so good.

definitions.definition: To represent in or with a picture.
To imagine or envision.
To depict or describe vividly.
Cinema, movie theatre
definitions.example: We go to the pictures every Saturday night.

definitions.definition: Generosity in the giving of gifts or money.
The gifts or money given in such a way.
A benevolent demeanor.
A very large outdoor sign, generally used for advertising.
A flat surface, such as a panel or fence, on which bills are posted; a bulletin board.
A piece of thick plank, armed with iron plates, and fixed on the bow or fore-channels of a vessel, for the bill or fluke of the anchor to rest on.
A sprite that always faces the screen, no matter which direction it is looked at from.
A generally tubular invertebrate of the annelid phylum; an earthworm.
More loosely, any of various tubular invertebrates resembling annelids but not closely related to them, such as velvet worms, acorn worms, flatworms, or roundworms.
A type of wingless "dragon", especially a gigantic sea serpent.
Either a mythical "dragon" (especially wingless), a gigantic sea serpent, or a creature that resembles a Mongolian death worm.
A contemptible or devious being.
definitions.example: Don't try to run away, you little worm!

definitions.definition: A self-replicating program that propagates through a network.
A graphical representation of the total runs scored in an innings.
Anything helical, especially the thread of a screw.
Any creeping or crawling animal, such as a snake, snail, or caterpillar.
An internal tormentor; something that gnaws or afflicts one’s mind with remorse.
A strip of linked tiles sharing parallel edges in a tiling.
The lytta.
(preceded by definite article) A dance, or dance move, in which the dancer lies on the floor and undulates the body horizontally thereby moving forwards.
To make (one's way) with a crawling motion.
definitions.example: We wormed our way through the underbrush.

definitions.definition: To move with one's body dragging the ground.
To work one's way by artful or devious means.
To work (one's way or oneself) (into) gradually or slowly; to insinuate.
definitions.example: He wormed his way into the organization

definitions.definition: To effect, remove, drive, draw, or the like, by slow and secret means; often followed by out.
(in “worm out of”) To drag out of, to get information that someone is reluctant or unwilling to give (through artful or devious means or by pleading or asking repeatedly).
To fill in the contlines of (a rope) before parcelling and serving.
definitions.example: Worm and parcel with the lay; turn and serve the other way.

definitions.definition: To deworm (an animal).
To cut the worm, or lytta, from under the tongue of (a dog, etc.) for the purpose of checking a disposition to gnaw, and formerly supposed to guard against canine madness.
To clean by means of a worm; to draw a wad or cartridge from, as a firearm.
Any shrub or tree of the genus Juniperus of the cypress family, which is characterized by pointed, needle-like leaves and aromatic berry-like cones.
One of a number of coniferous trees which resemble junipers.
Gin.
A minor distinction.
Subtlety or fine detail.
definitions.example: Understanding the basics is easy, but appreciating the nuances takes years.

definitions.definition: To apply a nuance to; to change or redefine in a subtle way.
To succeed in something, now especially in academic performance.
To carry out successfully; to accomplish.
To conclude, finish, especially successfully.
To obtain, or gain (a desired result, objective etc.), as the result of exertion; to succeed in gaining; to win.
To conclude, to turn out.
To obtain (a material thing).
definitions.example: Show all the spoils by valiant kings achieved.

definitions.definition: (of a goal or status) Having been reached, attained or accomplished.
The flat, roundish bone in the knee.
(roofing) A metal cover trim that fits over a panel rib after it has been cut and bent.
A cap or strong covering for the knees, used chiefly for horses, to protect their knees in case of a fall.
To destroy the knees of (a person), usually by shooting at the knees, as a punishment carried out by criminals or terrorists.
A human being, whether man, woman or child.
definitions.example: Humans share common ancestors with other apes.

definitions.definition: Able to be spoken of; able to be expressed.
An official in charge of payments to employees, troops, etc.
A person or body which demands loyalty or services in return for payment (especially as paid in advance).

In such writing systems as the Chinese writing system, the portion of a phono-semantic character that provides an indication of its pronunciation; contrasted with semantic (which is usually the radical).
Relating to the sounds of spoken language.
Relating to phones (as opposed to phonemes)
A professional person qualified (as by a law degree or bar exam) and authorized to practice law, i.e. represent parties in lawsuits or trials and give legal advice.
definitions.example: A lawyer's time and advice are his stock in trade. - aphorism often credited to Abraham Lincoln, but without attestation

definitions.definition: (by extension) A legal layman who argues points of law.
The burbot.
The stem of a bramble.
To practice law.
To perform, or attempt to perform, the work of a lawyer.
To make legalistic arguments.
To barrage (a person) with questions in order to get them to admit something.
definitions.example: You've been lawyered!

definitions.definition: To arrange or score music for performance by an orchestra.
To compose or arrange orchestral music for a dramatic performance.
definitions.example: Sergio Leone orchestrated "The Good, the Bad and the Ugly".

definitions.definition: To arrange or direct diverse elements to achieve a desired effect
definitions.example: Orchestrated histories are not fact-based.

definitions.definition: A semiaquatic rodent of the genus Castor, having a wide, flat tail and webbed feet.
A hat, of various shapes, made from a felted beaver fur (or later of silk), fashionable in Europe between 1550 and 1850.
The pubic hair and/or vulva of a woman.
The fur of the beaver.
Beaver cloth, a heavy felted woollen cloth, used chiefly for making overcoats.
A brown colour, like that of a beaver (also called beaver brown).
A man who wears a beard.
The vapor formed when water changes from liquid phase to gas phase.
Pressurized water vapour used for heating, cooking, or to provide mechanical energy.
Internal energy for motive power.
definitions.example: After three weeks in bed he was finally able to sit up under his own steam.

definitions.definition: Pent-up anger.
definitions.example: Dad had to go outside to blow off some steam.

definitions.definition: A steam-powered vehicle.
Travel by means of a steam-powered vehicle.
Any exhalation.
Fencing without the use of any electric equipment.
To cook with steam.
definitions.example: The best way to cook artichokes is to steam them.

definitions.definition: To expose to the action of steam; to apply steam to for softening, dressing, or preparing.
definitions.example: to steam wood or cloth

definitions.definition: To produce or vent steam.
To rise in vapour; to issue, or pass off, as vapour.
definitions.example: Our breath steamed in the cold winter air.

definitions.definition: To become angry; to fume; to be incensed.
To make angry.
definitions.example: It really steams me to see her treat him like that.

definitions.definition: To be covered with condensed water vapor.
definitions.example: With all the heavy breathing going on the windows were quickly steamed in the car.

definitions.definition: To travel by means of steam power.
definitions.example: The ship steamed out of the harbour

definitions.definition: To move with great or excessive purposefulness.
definitions.example: If he heard of anyone picking the fruit he would steam off and lecture them.

definitions.definition: To exhale.
Old-fashioned; from before the digital age.
An individual member of the Mohawk people.
(also mohawk) A hairstyle where both sides are shaved, with the hair along the crest of the head kept long, and usually styled so as to stand straight up.
A member of a gang (the Mohocks) that terrorized London in the early 18th century.
(gaming slang) micromanagement
(gaming slang) to micromanage
Small, relatively small; used to contrast levels of the noun modified.
definitions.example: At the micro level he was a good manager. At the macro level he failed.

definitions.definition: A computer designed around a microprocessor, smaller than a minicomputer or a mainframe.
The field of economics that deals with small-scale economic activities such as those of an individual or company.
A cut made in the lower part of something; the material so removed.
The notch cut in a tree to direct its fall when being felled.
The underside of a sirloin of beef; the fillet.
A hairstyle that is shaved or clipped short on the sides and kept long on the top.
A blow dealt upward.
To sell (something) at a lower price, or to work for lower wages, than a competitor.
To create an overhang by cutting away material from underneath.
To undermine.
To strike a heavy blow upward.
Produced by undercutting.
Designed so as to cut from the underside.
Having the parts in relief cut under.
The organic process by which an organism assimilates food and uses it for growth and maintenance.
That which nourishes; nutriment.
To make ashamed; to embarrass; to destroy the self-possession of, as by exciting suddenly a consciousness of guilt, mistake, or inferiority; to disconcert; to discomfit.
To lose self-possession; to become ashamed.
Embarrassed, disconcerted, or ashamed.
To turn thread or yarn into a piece of fabric by forming loops that are pulled through each other. This can be done by hand with needles or by machine.
definitions.example: The first generation knitted to order; the second still knits for its own use; the next leaves knitting to industrial manufacturers.

definitions.definition: To join closely and firmly together.
definitions.example: The fight for survival knitted the men closely together.

definitions.definition: To become closely and firmly joined; become compacted.
To grow together.
definitions.example: All those seedlings knitted into a kaleidoscopic border.

definitions.definition: To combine from various elements.
definitions.example: The witness knitted together his testimony from contradictory pieces of hearsay.

definitions.definition: Of bones: to heal following a fracture.
definitions.example: I’ll go skiing again after my bones knit.

definitions.definition: To form into a knot, or into knots; to tie together, as cord; to fasten by tying.
To draw together; to contract into wrinkles.
Made by knitting, or resembling in texture something made by knitting.
So as to form a circle or trace a circular path, or approximation thereof.
definitions.example: High above, vultures circled around.

definitions.definition: So as to surround or be near.
definitions.example: Everybody please gather around.

definitions.definition: Nearly; approximately; about.
definitions.example: An adult elephant weighs around five tons.

definitions.definition: From place to place.
definitions.example: Look around and see what you find.

definitions.definition: From one state or condition to an opposite or very different one; with a metaphorical change in direction; bringing about awareness or agreement.
definitions.example: He used to stay up late but his new girlfriend changed that around.

definitions.definition: (with turn, spin etc.) So as to partially or completely rotate; so as to face in the opposite direction.
definitions.example: She spun around a few times.

definitions.definition: Used with verbs to indicate repeated or continuous action, or in numerous locations or with numerous people.
definitions.example: I asked around, and no-one really liked it.

definitions.definition: Used with certain verbs to suggest unproductive activity.
Defining a circle or closed curve containing a thing.
definitions.example: I planted a row of lilies around the statue.

definitions.definition: (of abstract things) Centred upon; surrounding.
definitions.example: There has been a lot of controversy around the handling of personal information.

definitions.definition: Following the perimeter of a specified area and returning to the starting point.
definitions.example: She went around the track fifty times.

definitions.definition: Following a path which curves near an object, with the object on the inside of the curve.
definitions.example: The road took a brief detour around the large rock formation, then went straight on.

definitions.definition: Near; in the vicinity of.
definitions.example: I don't want you around me.

definitions.definition: At or to various places within.
definitions.example: She went around the office and got everyone to sign the card.

definitions.definition: A circular or spherical object or part of an object.
A circular or repetitious route.
definitions.example: The guards have started their rounds; the prisoner should be caught soon.

definitions.definition: A general outburst from a group of people at an event.
definitions.example: The candidate got a round of applause after every sentence or two.

definitions.definition: A song that is sung by groups of people with each subset of people starting at a different time.
A serving of something; a portion of something to each person in a group.
definitions.example: They brought us a round of drinks about every thirty minutes.

definitions.definition: A single individual portion or dose of medicine.
One sandwich (two full slices of bread with filling).
A long-bristled, circular-headed paintbrush used in oil and acrylic painting.
A firearm cartridge, bullet, or any individual ammunition projectile. Originally referring to the spherical projectile ball of a smoothbore firearm. Compare round shot and solid shot.
One of the specified pre-determined segments of the total time of a sport event, such as a boxing or wrestling match, during which contestants compete before being signaled to stop.
A stage, level, set of events in a game
(drafting, CAD) A rounded relief or cut at an edge, especially an outside edge, added for a finished appearance and to soften sharp edges.
A strip of material with a circular face that covers an edge, gap, or crevice for decorative, sanitary, or security purposes.
definitions.example: All furniture in the nursery had rounds on the edges and in the crevices.

definitions.definition: (butchery) The hindquarters of a bovine.
A rung, as of a ladder.
A crosspiece that joins and braces the legs of a chair.
A series of changes or events ending where it began; a series of like events recurring in continuance; a cycle; a periodical revolution.
definitions.example: the round of the seasons    a round of pleasures

definitions.definition: A course of action or conduct performed by a number of persons in turn, or one after another, as if seated in a circle.
A series of duties or tasks which must be performed in turn, and then repeated.
A circular dance.
Rotation, as in office; succession.
A general discharge of firearms by a body of troops in which each soldier fires once.
An assembly; a group; a circle.
definitions.example: a round of politicians

definitions.definition: A brewer's vessel in which the fermentation is concluded, the yeast escaping through the bunghole.
A vessel filled, as for drinking.
A round-top.
A round of beef.
To shape something into a curve.
definitions.example: The carpenter rounded the edges of the table.

definitions.definition: To become shaped into a curve.
(with "out") To finish; to complete; to fill out.
definitions.example: She rounded out her education with only a single mathematics class.

definitions.definition: To approximate a number, especially a decimal number by the closest whole number.
definitions.example: Ninety-five rounds up to one hundred.

definitions.definition: To turn past a boundary.
definitions.example: Helen watched him until he rounded the corner.

definitions.definition: To turn and attack someone or something (used with on).
definitions.example: As a group of policemen went past him, one of them rounded on him, grabbing him by the arm.

definitions.definition: To advance to home plate.
definitions.example: And the runners round the bases on the double by Jones.

definitions.definition: To go round, pass, go past.
To encircle; to encompass.
To grow round or full; hence, to attain to fullness, completeness, or perfection.
To do ward rounds.
To go round, as a guard; to make the rounds.
To go or turn round; to wheel about.
(physical) Shape.
Complete, whole, not lacking.
definitions.example: The baker sold us a round dozen.

definitions.definition: (of a number) Convenient for rounding other numbers to; for example, ending in a zero.
definitions.example: One hundred is a nice round number.

definitions.definition: Pronounced with the lips drawn together; rounded.
Outspoken; plain and direct; unreserved; not mincing.
definitions.example: a round answer; a round oath

definitions.definition: Finished; polished; not defective or abrupt; said of authors or their writing style.
Consistent; fair; just; applied to conduct.
Large in magnitude.
definitions.example: a round sum

A young duck.

A special form of secured loan where the purpose of the loan must be specified to the lender, to purchase assets that must be fixed (not movable) property, such as a house or piece of farm land. The assets are registered as the legal property of the borrower but the lender can seize them and dispose of them if they are not satisfied with the manner in which the repayment of the loan is conducted by the borrower. Once the loan is fully repaid, the lender loses this right of seizure and the assets are then deemed to be unencumbered.

definitions.definition: (by extension) Imperial power, because the colour purple was worn by emperors and kings.
Any of various species of mollusks from which Tyrian purple dye was obtained, especially the common dog whelk.
The purple haze cultivar of cannabis in the kush family, either pure or mixed with others, or by extension any variety of smoked marijuana.
Purpura.
Earcockle, a disease of wheat.
Any of the species of large butterflies, usually marked with purple or blue, of the genus Basilarchia (formerly Limenitis).
definitions.example: the banded purple

definitions.definition: A cardinalate.
To turn purple in colour.
To dye purple.
To clothe in purple.
Having a colour/color that is a dark blend of red and blue.
Not predominantly red or blue, but having a mixture of Democrat and Republican support, as in purple state, purple city.
(in Netherlands and Belgium) Mixed between social democrats and liberals.
Imperial; regal.
Blood-red; bloody.
(of language) Extravagantly ornate, like purple prose.
(of a sector, lap, etc.) Completed in the fastest time so far in a given session.
A recreational drug based on cough syrup, popular in the hip-hop community in the southern United States.
To make such a sudden, unsteady movement.
To swallow or eat greedily; to devour; hence, to swallow up.
To leave someone in the lurch; to cheat.
To rob.
To evade by stooping; to lurk.
Commodity that is shoddy or inferior.
Things that are new.
definitions.example: Out with the old, in with the new.

definitions.definition: A kind of light beer.
A naval cadet who has just embarked on training.
To make new; to recreate; to renew.
Recently made, or created.
definitions.example: This is a new scratch on my car!   The band just released a new album.

definitions.definition: Additional; recently discovered.
definitions.example: We turned up some new evidence from the old files.

definitions.definition: Current or later, as opposed to former.
definitions.example: My new car is much better than my previous one, even though it is older.   We had been in our new house for five years by then.

definitions.definition: Used to distinguish something established more recently, named after something or some place previously existing.
definitions.example: New Bond Street is an extension of Bond Street.

definitions.definition: In original condition; pristine; not previously worn or used.
definitions.example: Are you going to buy a new car or a second-hand one?

definitions.definition: Refreshed, reinvigorated, reformed.
definitions.example: That shirt is dirty. Go and put on a new one.   I feel like a new person after a good night's sleep.   After the accident, I saw the world with new eyes.

definitions.definition: Newborn.
definitions.example: My sister has a new baby, and our mother is excited to finally have a grandchild.

definitions.definition: Of recent origin; having taken place recently.
definitions.example: I can't see you for a while; the pain is still too new.   Did you see the new King Lear at the theatre?

definitions.definition: Strange, unfamiliar or not previously known.
definitions.example: The idea was new to me.   I need to meet new people.

definitions.definition: Recently arrived or appeared.
definitions.example: Have you met the new guy in town?   He is the new kid at school.

definitions.definition: Inexperienced or unaccustomed at some task.
definitions.example: Don't worry that you're new at this job; you'll get better with time.   I'm new at this business.

definitions.definition: (of a period of time) Next; about to begin or recently begun.
definitions.example: We expect to grow at 10% annually in the new decade.

definitions.definition: Newly (especially in composition).
definitions.example: new-born, new-formed, new-found, new-mown

definitions.definition: As new; from scratch.
definitions.example: They are scraping the site clean to build new.

definitions.definition: To create (an object) by calling its constructor.
